dialogos.texto_en: (Uesugi-kun went to Tokyo,\n and my university life began...\n It was a dizzying time for a while after that.)
(University and high school are completely different\n things. It's a time when you're constantly thinking\n about what you want to do, why you're studying...)
(But, I have dreams, and goals.\n Above all, there are people who support me%p-2;――%p;)
Phew...
(I'm still not used to writing reports. It's not so\n much about solving problems as it is about\n organizing information and writing your own thoughts.)
Nnn...
(I've lost my focus.\n Should I take a break here?)
Itsuki, you've been working hard.
Oh, Miku.\nSorry for taking up the table.
It's okay. It's not like it's something new.
Well, I can't concentrate in my room sometimes,\nand I wanted to change the environment and take a break.
Reports are tough, aren't they.\nHave you made any progress?
Not really...
But, I'm enjoying it.\nEven the struggle to put my thoughts into words.
Yotsuba looks like she's dying as she writes them.
Ahaha...\nWell, everyone is different.
But pushing yourself too hard\njust because you enjoy it isn't good.\nI brought some drinks, so let's take a break.
Thank you! I was just thinking of taking a break too.
Is barley tea okay?\nI put it in your usual glass.
...Heheh.
(This glass that I exchanged with Uesugi-kun,\n no matter how many times I look at it%p-2;――%p;)
Do you really like it that much?
Yes, I really, really do.
It's a bit crooked, isn't it...\nIs it perhaps handmade?\nDid you make it yourself, Itsuki?
No, it's not like that.\nBut don't you think this shape\nhas its own unique taste?
Well, I guess you could call it a taste...
(As Miku said, it's a bit awkward, but it has\n a warmth that can only come from something handmade.\n I can feel his presence in it, too...)
(Plus, this colorful dot pattern\n that's mixed with five colors%p-2;――%p;)
Really, it's these kinds of things that...
Hm? What?
Oh, nothing. I was just thinking\nhow much I like this glass.
I can really tell...
(Whenever I look at this glass,\n I always feel you by my side, Uesugi-kun.)
...Well then!
Are you going to continue?\nYou said you'd take a break.
I think I'll push on a little harder.\nSeeing the glass has given me energy.
Don't push yourself too hard, okay?\nI'm going to my room, then.
Okay. Thanks for the drink.
(Uesugi-kun must still be working hard.\n Then I'll work hard with him%p-2;――%p;)
That's right... Futaro.
